name: database
context: Query Supabase Postgres — select, insert, update, delete, filters, joins, RPC, and the PostgREST query builder
info: Supabase exposes your Postgres database through PostgREST, generating a REST API from your schema automatically. The @supabase/supabase-js client provides a query builder: `supabase.from('table')` chains `.select()`, `.insert()`, `.update()`, `.upsert()`, `.delete()` with powerful filters (`.eq()`, `.gt()`, `.in()`, `.like()`, `.textSearch()`, etc.), ordering, pagination, and relationship queries via foreign key joins. For complex logic, use `.rpc()` to call Postgres functions. All queries respect Row Level Security policies when using the anon key. The query builder returns `{ data, error, count, status }`. Queries are typed when using generated Database types from [[supabase/cli]].

sections.title: Select queries
sections.body: `supabase.from('table').select('columns')` reads data. Use `*` for all columns, comma-separated names for specific columns. Chain filters, ordering, and pagination. Use `.single()` for exactly one row, `.maybeSingle()` for zero or one. Pass `{ count: 'exact' }` as the second arg to get a row count alongside data.

sections.title: Insert, update, upsert, delete
sections.body: `.insert()` creates rows. `.update()` modifies rows matching filters. `.upsert()` inserts or updates based on conflict columns. `.delete()` removes rows matching filters. Always chain `.select()` after mutations to get affected rows back. Without filters on update/delete, PostgREST rejects the query to prevent accidental bulk operations.

sections.title: Filter operators
sections.body: Filters chain onto select/update/delete: `.eq()`, `.neq()`, `.gt()`, `.gte()`, `.lt()`, `.lte()`, `.like()`, `.ilike()`, `.in()`, `.is()`, `.contains()`, `.containedBy()`, `.textSearch()`. Use `.or()` for OR conditions with PostgREST syntax: `.or('col.eq.a,col.eq.b')`. Filters on joined tables use dot notation: `.eq('comments.status', 'approved')`.

sections.title: Foreign key joins
sections.body: PostgREST detects foreign keys and enables embedding related tables in `.select()`. Syntax: `select('*, comments(id, body)')`. Use `!inner` for INNER JOIN behavior: `select('*, comments!inner(id)')`. Rename with aliasing: `author:profiles(name)`. Nested joins work to any depth. If the foreign key is ambiguous, specify it: `comments!post_id(id)`.

sections.title: RPC — Postgres functions
sections.body: Call Postgres functions with `supabase.rpc('function_name', { args })`. Functions must be created in SQL and be in the `public` schema (or whichever schema PostgREST exposes). Use for complex queries, aggregations, transactions, or any logic that's better expressed in SQL. Functions can return single values, rows, or sets of rows.
